back_translation: The LORD said to Moses, 'Chisel for yourself two stone tablets like the first ones, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets that you broke.'
'Be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning to Mount Sinai and present yourself before me there on the top of the mountain.'
'No man shall come up with you, and no man shall be seen on the whole mountain; also let no flocks or herds graze in front of that mountain.'
He chiseled two stone tablets like the first ones, and Moses rose early in the morning and went up to Mount Sinai as the LORD commanded him, taking in his hand the two stone tablets.
The LORD came down in the cloud and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name of the LORD.
The LORD passed before his face and proclaimed: 'The LORD, the LORD, God compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness,'

'keeping steadfast love for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, but who will by no means clear the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children and the children's children to the third and the fourth generation.'
Moses hastened and bowed his head to the ground and worshiped.

He said, 'If please I have found favor in your eyes, my Lord, let my Lord please go in our midst, for it is a stiff-necked people; and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us as your inheritance.'

He said, 'Behold, I am making a covenant. Before all your people I will do wonders that have not been created in all the earth and in any of the nations; and all the people in whose midst you are shall see the work of the LORD, for it is an awesome thing that I am doing with you.'
'Observe for yourself what I command you today. Behold, I am driving out from before you the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.'
'Take heed for yourself, lest you make a covenant with the inhabitant of the land that you are entering, lest it become a snare in your midst.'
'But you shall tear down their altars and shatter their sacred stones, and cut down their Asherah poles.'
'For you shall not bow down to another god, for the LORD whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.'
'Lest you make a covenant with the inhabitant of the land, and they prostitute themselves after their gods and sacrifice to their gods, and one invite you, and you eat of his sacrifice.'
'And you take of his daughters for your sons, and his daughters prostitute themselves after their gods, and they cause your sons to prostitute themselves after their gods.'
'Molten gods you shall not make for yourself.'

'You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in the month of Abib you came out of Egypt.'
'Every firstborn of the womb is mine, and every male from your livestock — the firstborn of ox or sheep.'

'The firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb; and if you do not redeem it, you shall break its neck. Every firstborn of your sons you shall redeem; and none shall appear before me empty-handed.'
'Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest. In plowing and harvesting you shall rest.'
'And the Feast of Weeks you shall observe — the firstfruits of the wheat harvest — and the Feast of Ingathering at the turn of the year.'
'Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the face of the Lord, the LORD, the God of Israel.'
'For I will dispossess nations from before you and enlarge your border. No man shall covet your land when you go up to appear before the face of the LORD your God three times in the year.'
'You shall not slaughter the blood of my sacrifice with leaven, and the sacrifice of the Feast of Passover shall not remain until morning.'
'The best of the firstfruits of your ground you shall bring to the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.'
The LORD said to Moses, 'Write for yourself these words, for according to these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.'
He was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did not eat bread and did not drink water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant — the Ten Words.

When Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the Testimony in Moses' hand, as he came down from the mountain, Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone because he had been speaking with him.
Aaron and all the sons of Israel saw Moses, and behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to approach him.
Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him; and Moses spoke to them.
Afterward all the sons of Israel approached, and he commanded them all that the LORD had spoken to him on Mount Sinai.
When Moses finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face.
Whenever Moses went in before the LORD to speak with him, he would remove the veil until he came out; then he would come out and tell the sons of Israel what he had been commanded.
The sons of Israel would see the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses' face shone; and Moses would put the veil back on his face until he went in to speak with him.
